questions.question: Which of the following is a technique used to create new features from existing ones that can lead to better performance in machine learning models?
options: A) Feature Scaling
B) One-Hot Encoding
C) Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
D) Standardization
questions.explanation: PCA is a technique used to reduce the dimensionality of a dataset while retaining most of the information. It creates new features called principal components.

questions.question: What is the main goal of dimensionality reduction techniques?
options: A) To increase the number of features in a dataset
B) To decrease the number of features in a dataset while preserving the most important information
C) To reduce noise in a dataset
D) To increase the accuracy of machine learning models
questions.explanation: The main goal of dimensionality reduction techniques is to decrease the number of features in a dataset while preserving the most important information.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of using PCA for feature engineering?
options: A) It can lead to information loss
B) It can increase the risk of overfitting
C) It can only be used for linear relationships
D) It can only be used for non-linear relationships
questions.explanation: PCA can lead to information loss because it does not preserve all of the information in the original dataset.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Standardization in feature engineering?
options: A) To scale the data to a specific range
B) To normalize the data to a specific range
C) To reduce the dimensionality of the data
D) To remove noise from the data
questions.explanation: Standardization is a technique used to scale the data to have a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1, which can improve the performance of some machine learning algorithms.

questions.question: Which of the following dimensionality reduction techniques is suitable for high-dimensional data with non-linear relationships?
options: A) PCA
B) t-SNE
C) Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA)
D) Principal Regression
questions.explanation: t-SNE (t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding) is a dimensionality reduction technique that is suitable for high-dimensional data with non-linear relationships.
